titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Maxine Kumin Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 4255075.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday October 8, 2020 at 03:02

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains writings, correspondence, and other materials documenting Maxine Kumin's literary work and professional and public activities since 1960.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 47, 54, 61, and 64-65 (audiovisual material, computer disks): Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 43-46 and 62-63 (sound and video recordings): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 79, 82, 94 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 80, 81, 96, 98 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James S. Jaffe Books on various funds, 1990-2015.
May 2017 Acquisition: Purchased from Maxine W. Kumin Literary Trust on the George B. Alvord Fund, 2017.
June 2017 Acquisition: Gift of Susannah Colt, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into ten groupings: March 1990 Acquisition; May 1995 Acquisition; March 2001 Acquisition; January 2005 Acquisition; August 2006 Acquisition; January 2012 Acquisition; March 2013 Acquisition; September 2015 Acquisition; May 2017 Acquisition; and June 2017 Acquisition.

bioghist.head: Maxine Kumin (1925-)
bioghist.p: Maxine Kumin was born Maxine Winokur on 6 June 1925 in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania and educated at Radcliffe College (1946, 1948). Kumin worked as an instructor and lecturer in English and Tufts University for several years between 1958 and 1968 and has held visting appointments at other institutions around the country since the 1970s. She is the author of numerous children's books, poetry collections, essay and short story collections, and novels, and her many awards include the Pulitzer Prize (1973) for Up Country , American Academy and Institute of Arts and Letters Award for excellence in literature (1980), Poets' Prize (1994) and Aiken Talyor Poetry Prize (1995) for Looking For Luck , and Ruth Lilly Poetry Prize (1999). Since 1976, she and her husband have lived on a farm in Warner, New Hampshire.
Biographical information taken from "Maxine Kumin." Contemporary Women Poets . Gale, 1998. Biography In Context . Web. 13 June 2013.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Maxine Kumin Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and in some instances minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below according to month and year of acquisition.

The finding aid for this collection is compiled from individual preliminary lists for each acquisition that were created at or around the time of receipt by the library. The preliminary lists were migrated to comply with current archival descriptive standards and merged into a single file in 2007-2008.

As a rule, descriptive information found in the Collection Contents section is drawn in large part from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.
Former call numbers: Uncat Za Ms 131, Uncat Za Ms 436, Uncat MSS 118, Uncat MSS 739, and Uncat MSS 899.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains writings, correspondence, and other materials documenting Maxine Kumin's literary work and professional and public activities since 1960. Writings include drafts, proofs, and other materials relating to book-length and shorter works. Kumin wrote children's books, poetry collections, essay and short story collections, and novels, and files can be found for works dating from the early children's book A Winter Friend (1961) to the late collection of poems, Still to Mow (2007). Large files can be found for a variety of work: The Abduction (1971), The Long Approach (1985), Looking For Luck (1992), Women, Animals, and Vegetables (1994), Selected Poems 1960-1990 (1997), Quit Monks or Die (1999), Inside the Halo and Beyond the Journey (1999), Always Beginning (2000), and Jack and Other New Poems (2005).

Correspondence in the collection can be found with writers, literary critics, publishers, and family. Correspondents include: Dudley Fitts, William Meredith, Howard Nemerov, Anne Sexton, and the publishers Harper & Row, McGraw-Hill, W. W. Norton, Viking, and North Point Press. The March 2013 Acquisition contains a large file of correspondence, dating from 1945 and 1946, between Maxine Kumin and her husband Victor.

Other materials appear chiefly to document Kumin's professional and public actitivities. There are financial and legal records, such as contracts and permissions, correspondence and documents relating to speaking engagements and participation on literary juries and in the Bread Loaf Writers' Conference; printed materials, including reviews and publicity; audiovisual materials, including both sound and video recordings of readings by Kumin, interviews, and other cultural events; and personal papers, such as calendars and photographs.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Maxine Kumin Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: Bread Loaf Writers' Conference of Middlebury College

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James S. Jaffe on the Eugene G. O'Neill Memorial Fund and the Sinclair Lewis Fund, 1990.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Files are not arranged in any particular order.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Former call number: Uncat Za MS 131.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The March 1990 Acquisition includes writings, correspondence, and other professional and personal papers. Writings include drafts, proofs, and other materials relating to book-length and shorter works. Kumin wrote children's books, poetry collections, essay and short story collections, and novels, and files can be found for A Winter Friend (1961), The Abduction (1971), The Designated Heir (1974), and The Long Approach (1985), among others. There is also correspondence with writers, literary critics, and publishers, including Dudley Fitts, William Meredith, Howard Nemerov, Anne Sexton, Atlantic Monthly , Poetry Magazine , Saturday Review , Harper and Row, McGraw-Hill, and W. W. Norton. Other materials chiefly document Kumin's professional actitivities, such as speaking engagements and participation on literary juries and in the Bread Loaf Writers' Conference.

title.part: Why Can't We Live Together Like Civilized Human Beings?
Our Ground Time Here Will Be Brief

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James S. Jaffe on the Eugene G. O'Neill Memorial Fund, 1995.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Files are not arranged in any particular order.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Former call number: Uncat Za Ms 436.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The May 1995 acquisition contains correspondence, writings, and personal papers by or relating to Maxine Kumin. Writings include drafts of the story "A Durable Solution" and other short and unsorted works. There is correspondence with writers and friends, Curtis Brown, Viking and North Point Press publishers, and others. Other materials include financial records, clippings, recommendations, and material relating to speaking engagements.

did.unittitle: Friends, old; non-poets; friends of family
Students, would-be writers etc. from speaking engagements
Friends who are primarily writers--frequent correspondence
Students, would-be writers from speaking engagements
Excerpts from other writers--to use in teaching
PEN American Center, also Poets & Writers
Poets & Place--draft and xerox--Massachusetts Council on the Humanities lecture
Children's story--work in process--worksheets, kids' poems
Recomendations and blurbs, also serving as judge
Permissions to reprint poems, anthologies, tapes
Requests for poems, articles, reprint rights
The Retrieval System (correspondence with Viking, etc.)
Students, would-be writers from speaking engagements

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James S. Jaffe on the Eugene G. O'Neill Memorial Fund, 2001.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Files are not arranged in any particular order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The March 2001 acquisition contains writings, correspondence, and professional and personal papers dating predominantly from the 1990s. In addition to large files for the poetry collection Looking For Luck (1992), and the collections Women, Animals, and Vegetables (1994) and Inside the Halo and Beyond the Journey (1999), the group includes shorter writings, financial and legal records relating to publications, such as contracts and permissions, and publicity and reviews.

did.unittitle: Students, would-be writers, from speakers, engagements, etc.
Students, would-be writers from speaking engagements

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 43-46 (sound and video recordings): Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 47 (computer disk): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James S. Jaffe on the Elizabeth Wakeman Dwight Memorial Fund, 2005.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Correspondence, Writings, Miscellaneous, and Audiovisual Material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The January 2005 Acquisition contains additional correspondence, writings, personal papers, and audiovisual material. In addition to large files of family correspondence, material relating to the poetry collection The Long Marriage (2001), and calendars, the group features sound recordings of poetry readings by Kumin and interviews.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Donald Hall, Josephine Jacobsen, Daniel Hoffman, and William Jay Smith.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Carolyn Kizer, Daniel Hoffman, Josephine Jacobsen, Donald Hall, and Robert Pinsky

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes corrected typescripts; corrected printer's proof, first pass; corrected printer's proof, second pass; original black and white photograph (used as frontispiece); original mock-up for book, with corrections; corrected printer's proof, third pass

Clippings, etc. related to Saul Simon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All video recordings are VHS format

did.unittitle: "Bookmark (CSPAN), Donald Hall with Edward Hirsch and Maxine Kumin"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chronicle, 1986 January 30, Maxine's interview, about 30 minutes (5 minutes Maxine); WH Crossroads 1990 May 25, Maxine interview, about 30 minutes (5-10 minutes Maxine); Judith in ABC interview Fr. Belgrade 1992 May 19/David Carroll Chronicle 1994 May 25

"Yankee Cable Network, Kearsarge Valley Magazine, Interview with MK"
"North of Boston: Readings by NH Poets, Jane Kenyon, Donald Hall, Maxine Kumin, Charles Simic"

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.p: Box 54 (sound and video recordings, computer disk): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James S. Jaffe on the Edwin J. Beinecke Fund, 2006.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Files are not arranged in any particular order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The August 2006 Acquisition contains correspondence, writings, printed material, personal papers, and sound and video recordings. There are large files of drafts and proofs of Selected Poems 1960-1990 (1997), Always Beginning (2000), Quit Monks or Die (1999), and Jack and Other New Poems (2005). Additional professional materials relate to speaking engagements, recommendations, and awards, such as honorary degrees. There are also sound and video recordings of Kumin readings and other activities.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James S. Jaffe on the Elizabeth Wakeman Dwight Memorial Fund, 2012.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Writings, Other Papers, and Audiovisual Material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The January 2012 Acquisition contains writings, other papers, and audiovisual material. Writings include drafts and other materials relating to books published from the late 1990s onward. Other papers include correspondence, printed materials, financial and legal records, material documenting Kumin's professional and public engagements, and personal papers. Audiovisual materials document readings by Kumin and other cultural events.

title.part: The Wonderful Babies of 1809 and Other Years

scopecontent.p: Includes photographs and drawings

accessrestrict.p: Computer disks are restricted. Copies of electronic files may be requested through Access Services

did.unittitle: "Poetry Session", Hayden Library Lounge at M.I.T.
did.materialspec: The program is distorted at times. Low-level cross talk can be heard at times.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poets included in the session are listed.

did.materialspec: The program is distorted at times. The program begins abruptly.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: "Transformations", opera by Anne Sexton and Conrad Susa
did.materialspec: The program is distorted at times. Low-level cross talk can be heard at times. Static can be heard on the tape. The left channel was recorded at a lower level for about five minutes at 01:01:00.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contents of this audiotape reel are listed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: There is a letter included with this audiotape reel. It reads: "Dear Max, So finally here 'tis. I had it reproduced by a professional recording company in its entirety, just as I had taped it originally off the radio. So you'll get not only the opera itself, but tid-bits of "This is KPFA-KPFB Berkeley", etc., not to mention an interview between the two sections of the opera between a Dennis somebody and Phillip Brunel, complete with scattering of vapid remarks, but, also, some not so vapid discussion of the muicality, rhythms, Susa found inherent in Anne's poetry. As I recall, Anne told me she and Susa had not discussed the question of musical rhythms, ever, as applicable to the content of the poetry, & found some of same uncanny in their rightness; e.g., the almost bossa nova rhythm of the introductory passage of "Rapunzel", not to mention other instances throughout the work. Just who the guy is at the very end of side 2, someone who is being interviewed about his application to [to] met, etc., or why I recorded it, I know not. But I'm sure if you want any of the talky sections of the tape obliterated, old Dan can do it for you. Hope you are well. It was good to see you and Vic here in SF. That mare of yours must have foaled by now!? Please advise. Love, Annie

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: Static and a high amount of hum can be heard.

acqinfo.p: Purchased from James S. Jaffe on the Edwin J. Beinecke Fund, 2013.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The March 2013 Acquisition consists of correspondence between Maxine and Victor Kumin, dating between 1945 and 1946, and photographs.

did.unittitle: Letters from Maxine Kumin to Victor Kumin
Letters from Maxine Kumin to Victor Kumin
Letters from Victor Kumin to Maxine Kumin
Letters from Victor Kumin to Maxine Kumin

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition

did.unittitle: Letters from casual friends and fans
What Color Is Caesar? and other material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains newspaper clippings, publisher correspondence, and various drafts. Also contains material associated with Mites to Mastodons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts of "Metamorphosis: From Light Verse to the Poetry of Witness," "Love and War 1945-6," and "One Poet's View of Social Change at the Library of Congress."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains newspaper clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Donald Hall, Jeanne Braham, and Peter Schmitt. Also includes drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "Purim and the Beetles of Our Lady," "Sonnets Uncorseted," and other material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains magazines with articles about or by Maxine Kumin, including The New Yorker (2013 July 1), Lilith magazine (Summer 2013, Vol. 38 No. 2), and New Hampshire magazine (2013 May, Vol. 27 No. 5). Also includes a 2007 Old Farmer's Almanac Gardening calendar, and a William Stafford poetry broadside, entitled "B.C."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from Carole Oles, Hilda Raz, Jane Hirschfield, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains print-outs, photocopies and drafts.

did.unittitle: The Poetic Voice and the Romantic Tradition in the Poetry of Maxine Kumin, by Beverly D. Barton, masters thesis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From North Texas State University, Denton, Texas.

did.unittitle: Anyone for Tennyson?: An Introduction to Poetry in the English and American Tradition, program guide
Sandscript, by Cape Code Writers, Inc., No. 9

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes "The Circus Book: A Poetic Celebration in Honor of the Fifteenth Birthday of Poetry and Writers, Inc. (1985 December 5)," and "The Quarterly Journal of the Library of Congress (1982 Winter).

did.unittitle: Shakespeare's Sisters: Women Writers Bridge Five Centuries, edited by Gigi Bradford and Louisa Newlin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains "Grey Sparrow," Issue 3 (2010 Summer).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains handwritten events.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains blank Maxine Kumin postcards, related to "Always Beginning" and "Company of Dogs: 21 Stories by Contemporary Masters."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a photograph of Carole Oles and Maxine Kumin (1998 July 13), a handwritten note from Billy Collins (2013 October 6), a program from the funeral of Carol Houck Smith (2008), a postcard from David Ray to Maxine Kumin, a typed letter from Pamela Harrison, and other material from various people.

did.unittitle: The Pawnbroker's Daughter: A Memoir, typed drafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains typed drafts of other material, as well as emailed correspondence and photocopies with handwritten notes.

did.unittitle: Letters from students and would-be writers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters written to Maxine Kumin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters to Maxine Kumin, as well as a copy of Chiu Shui Poetry Quarterly, No. 148 (2011), with a placed in handwritten note.

did.unittitle: Letters from publishers and other writers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten notes removed from Still to Mow: Poems (2009), by Maxine Kumin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a draft for the front flap notes of And Short the Season: Poems (2015), associated email correspondence, and a Memorandum of Agreement regarding the book.

The Writer's Almanac - Week of June 3 - 9, 2013
Academy of American Poets - Robert Lowell and John Berryman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recorded 1963 October 13 at the Solomon R. Guggenheim Museum.

did.unittitle: Look to the Future - Ruth Stone, Reading from Ordinary Words and Simplicity
For the Waters and the Wild - Nan Webber, Reading the Early Poetry of William Butler Yeats
We Never Speak of It - Idaho Wyoming Poems, 1889-90 - Jana Harris

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten and typed notes removed from Where I Live: New & Selected Poems 1990-2010 (2011), by Maxine Kumin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a photocopy of "Making the Connection: the Nature Poetry of Maxine Kumin," by Alicia Ostriker, with handwritten notes by Maxine Kumin. Also contains a statement of sales and royalties regarding Lofty Dogmas from the University of Arkansas Press (2013).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes an Author's Agreement for "Our Farm, My Inspiration," a Maxine Kumin essay published in The American Scholar (2013).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains newspaper clippings, a copy of the State of New Hampshire Senate Journal (2014 March 6), and print-outs of Maxine Kumin's death annoucement from the New York Times, the Los Angeles Times, the Boston Globe, the Concord Monitor, and the Sentinel Source. Also includes a death notice for Susan Eisenberg (2013), a Register of Wills for the estate of Peter Winokur, and a copy of June 29, 1999 , by David Wiesner, used as a guest book for a 50th Wedding Anniversary of Maxine Kumin and Victor Kumin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains a photo strip of Maxine Kumin headshots.

did.unittitle: Letters form casual friends and fans

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Maxine Kumin's funeral card (2014 February 6), a photograph of Roger Laudrey (2006), an undated photograph of a woman and a horse, pickup instructions for the adoption of a dog named Rosita, a photograph of a puppy (possibly Rosita), donation cards made out in the memory of two horses ("Sailor" Jones and Boomer), various images of "Sailor" Jones and Boomer), some correspondence, a handmade John Muir bookmark, and a list of Maxine Kumine's works (circa 2006).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains publisher correspondence, as well publisher aggreements.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains condolence cards and notes regarding Maxine Kumin's death (2014 February), as well as Georgia Litwack's curriculum vitae.

did.unittitle: 2014 Annual Report of the Town of Warner, New Hampshire

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an In Memoriam for Maxin Kumin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Women's Review of Books, Vol. 32, Issue 1 (2015 January / February), The American Poetry Review, Vol. 44, No. 1 (2015 January / February), and clippings from the Concord Monitor (2014 May 7 and 2015 January 1).

did.unittitle: The Writer's Chronicle magazine, Vol. 47, No. 3
Visions, No. 35, Drew University Library, newsletter
WritingAmerica, National Endowment for the Arts, booklet
The University of Arkansas Press, catalog
Consultants' Reunion: A Keepsake Anthology of the Fiftieth Anniversary Celebration of the Consultantship in Poetry, The Library of Congress
Centaur magazine, Summer II, Vol. 1, No. 2
Radcliffe Class of 1946, Fiftieth Reunion, booklet
Inside the Halo and Beyond: The Anatomy of a Recovery, book cover

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes handwritten notes, a colored print-out of The Long Marriage book cover, a Concord Reads 2013 poster and with associated material, Maxine Kumin's self-inking address stamp, a "non-profit poet" button, and The Writer's Chronicle periodical, Vol. 43, No. 3 (2010 December).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains an official citation from the Massachusetts House of Representatives recognizing Maxine Kumin (1973 May 14), a William Stafford broadside containing a poem called "Smoke Signals - a dedication" (2011), a print-out of a Poetry Center at Smith poster for a reading by Maxine Kumin (undated), a diploma awarded to Maxine Kumin by the World Who's Who of Women (1973 May), a Marvin Bell broadside containing a poem called "Yes" (2011), a Tomas O'Leary broadside containing a poem called "Gödel's Proof" (2010), a handmade birthday poster for Victor Kumin (undated), and a "Women '76" mechanical (1976 April 22).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a copy of the Boston College Thursday Reporter Vol. 3, No. 8 (1973 December 6), a certificate awarded to Maxine Kumin for extra-curricular coursework from the Radcliffe Graduate School (1950), a photocopy of Maxine Kumin's entry in Who's Who (1968), a flier for a reading by Anne Sexton (circa 1970s), various letters from publishers, processed checks made out to Commentary magazine (1969), handwritten and typescript notes, postcards, poetry broadsides, a memorial booklet entitled "In Memory of Constance E. Smith (1970 December 10), a pencil portrait in profile of Maxine Kumin (1975 August), Women '76 booklet from the Boston YWCA (1976 April 22), booklets from the American Academy and Institute of Arts and Letters (1980), a program from the White House regarding a salute to poetry and American poets (1980 January 3), and other material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains newspaper clippings (1974; circa 2000s).

did.unittitle: Davis and Elkins College honorary degree, scrapbook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings and photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes the program for New England College's Thirty-Fifth Annual Commencement Excercises (1982).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains signed broadsides of "Parable" by Anne Marie Macari and "The Animal Messenger" by Jane Mead. Also contains a broadside entitled "Two Doves" which combines "A Voice's Gaze" by Li-Young Lee, "The Inkspots" by Gerald Stern, and an etching by Brian D. Cohen.

did.unittitle: Massachusetts House of Representatives, Congratulation Resolution for Maxine Kumin, certificate
Dartmouth College, Doctor of Letters degree certificate

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a Centre College of Kentucky Doctor of Humane Letters citation for Maxine Kumin (1976 May 30), a letter from bookseller Bert Babcock to Maxine Kumin regarding the return of a diploma (1997 December 19), Maxine Kumin's Centre College of Kentucky Doctor of Humane Letters diploma (1976 May 13), and a Radcliffe College diploma for Maxine Kumin (1948).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photocopied newspaper clipping from the Boston Sunday Globe, 1957 March 31, regarding the induction of Maxine Kumin as the 500th member of the Radcliffe Club of Boston. Shows the Radcliffe Club mug, being awarded as a prize.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains the Radcliffe Club mug, awarded as a prize to Maxine Kumin, for her membership in the Radcliffe Club of Boston, 1957 March 31.
